attestation.statement: Clinician confirmed in writing having clinically reviewed all 49 CVD (Cardiovascular) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)

codes_manifest.display: Short QT syndrome (candidate - other specified conduction disorders)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ provisional (from CDV-038) - maintainer to confirm/reassign within SPEC-CARD-05 bucket
difficulty_tier rare_condition (05) / diagnosis_category zebra_rare for the Short QT syndrome channelopathy; conservative tiering applied given SCD risk
age 24 and male stated in source (adult age_band)

telehealth reprojection applied - in-person after-hours home visit with cardiovascular/neurological examination and on-scene ambulance arranged reprojected to telehealth_chat; presentation layer de-anchored so 00/01/02 carry no diagnosis, tier, or red-flag labelling; abnormally short QTc on 12-lead ECG sealed to 10/11 as the diagnostic finding

clinician-measured normal vitals/exam (BP 115/75, HR 68, structurally normal heart, no murmur/click/heave, normal pulses) NOT carried into 01.objective_data_offered (clinician-obtained, not patient-obtainable); normal resting exam deliberately NOT used to downgrade urgency (SQTS is electrical, not structural)

SNOMED code 428417009 for Short QT syndrome is a best-effort candidate; ICD-10-AM I45.81 used as a candidate placeholder (other specified conduction disorders) - both flagged unverified for terminology receipt

no medications in source - quinidine recorded as specialist-initiated future antiarrhythmic adjunct (not_indicated_here); stimulants/QT-affecting agents encoded should_NOT_recommend; no dose fabricated
baseline tier T5 (immediate ambulance) consistent with source's ambulance dispatch and the sudden-death family history + arrhythmic-syncope risk; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T4
convulsive/arrhythmic syncope explicitly distinguished from epilepsy (rapid recovery, no tongue-biting/incontinence/post-ictal state) per source neurological findings
anchoring pitfall on the more familiar Long QT syndrome flagged - the diagnostic ECG hallmark here is an abnormally SHORT QTc
no PMH/allergies/guideline citation in source - left empty/omitted; comorbidities empty; empty arrays used where the source is silent

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
